steps.title: Use the sample mean
steps.content: Find the sample mean from the five acre counts: $$\frac{54+62+48+55+76}{5}=59$$ So the sample estimates about $59$ trees per acre.

steps.title: Scale to the whole forest
steps.content: Use the sample mean as the estimate for trees per acre, then multiply by the $247$ acres in the forest: $$59\times247=14{,}573$$

steps.title: Choose the interval
steps.content: $14{,}573$ falls between $14{,}000$ and $15{,}000$. That matches the computed answer, so C is correct.
